figures.caption: FIG. 1. Conceptual flowchart outlining the theoretical framework of this study. The “Simplicial Bridge” provides the exact mathematical mapping from the infinite-dimensional non-linear continuum (governed by PDEs) to a discrete, finite-dimensional hypergraph (governed by Kuramoto ODEs). This topological reduction isolates the multi-spin interactions necessary to predict the emergence of explosive synchronization and magnetic chimeras.

FIG. 2. Thermodynamic phase diagram demonstrating explosive synchronization. The curves represent the steady-state macroscopic order parameter R as a function of the bilinear exchange coupling K1. For purely pairwise interactions (K2 = 0.0, solid blue line), the system undergoes a continuous second-order phase transition. When triadic simplicial coupling is activated (K2 = 0.4), the transition becomes explosive (first-order), characterized by a stable upper branch (solid red line) and an unstable manifold (dashed red line). The shaded region delineates the bistable regime where the system exhibits a massive hysteresis loop bounded by the forward jump and backward collapse thresholds.
